<parameter=code>

</parameter>
</function>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see:
- id=6 (left green square): bnd=[[30, 30], [30, 32], [32, 32], [32, 30] - the green square moved from col 27-29 back to col 30-32. So the green square moved right by 3 columns.
- id=7 (right green square): bnd=[[30, 48], [30, 50], [32, 50], [32, 48] - the right green square is still at col 48-50.
- id=8: color=W pix=1 bnd=[[31, 31] - the white pixel inside the left green square is white.
- id=9: color=c pix=1 bnd=[[31, 49] - the charcoal pixel inside the right green square is charcoal.
So pressing RIGHT moved the left green square from col 27-29 back to col 30-32. The right green square stayed at col 48-50.
Wait, this is new! The left green square moved right, and the right green square stayed put. So the green squares can move independently.
Let me think about what the goal might be. The goal might be to get the green squares to be inside the dark red outlines. The dark red outlines are in the right room. The goal might be to get the green squares to be inside the dark red outlines.
Wait, the green squares are already in the right room. The goal might be to get the green squares to be inside the dark red outlines.
Let me try pressing RIGHT again to move the left green square further right.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try pressing RIGHT again to move the left green square further right
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
